name: Jitsi
license: Apache-2.0 / mixed component licenses across ecosystem
what: Open-source video conferencing platform for meetings and embedded calls.
solves: Video meetings, calls, and lightweight conferencing with self-host control.
users: Teams and product builders needing meetings or embeddable calls without classroom complexity.
fit: Better fit than BigBlueButton for general Hylono video consultations, team meetings, or lightweight session calls.
runtime: Multi-service media stack with web app, XMPP/JVB components, TURN/STUN, and optional recording/streaming add-ons.
components: Web UI, prosody/Jicofo/JVB, media relays, auth options, and deployment scripts.
deploy: Official Docker deployment is the practical path. Still requires media-capacity and TURN/STUN planning.
deps: Compute, bandwidth, TURN/STUN, storage for recordings if enabled, TLS, and auth integration.
auth: Anonymous, token, or integrated auth modes depending on setup.
apis: Meet APIs, iframe/embed, JWT auth, recording integrations, and media services.
security: Meeting access, media traffic, recording storage, and public exposure need careful configuration.
setup_path: Use official docker-jitsi-meet releases and handbook. Good candidate if Hylono needs self-hosted meetings but not full classroom tooling.
env: Public URL, TURN/STUN, auth/JWT, media ports, recording settings, and TLS.
persistence: Back up config and recording storage if recordings are used.
operations: Capacity, NAT/TURN, upgrades, and meeting-access controls matter.
failure_points: Underestimating media ops or exposing meetings without proper auth.
integration: Good fit for consultations, internal meetings, or embedded session calls. Could integrate with Cal.com booking and site flows.
verify: Confirm whether self-hosted video is actually needed versus managed meeting tools.
gdpr: Calls and recordings can be sensitive; consent and retention matter.
recommendation: PRIMARY SELF-HOST VIDEO CANDIDATE IF NEEDED
overlap: Preferred over BigBlueButton for general meetings.
codex_read_first: Read Jitsi and BBB together before recommending video infrastructure.
codex_best_path: Use Jitsi for simple meetings/consultations if self-hosting is really needed; otherwise avoid media ops burden.
codex_never_assume: Never assume self-hosted video is cheap to operate just because the software is open source.
